filters.predicate: (l_shipdate >= 8766 AND l_shipdate < 9131) AND (l_discount >= 5 AND l_discount <= 7) AND l_quantity < 2400

parallelism.threads: auto(nproc) with static contiguous row ranges

indexes.purpose: precheck candidate density; bypass block-pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap
precheck candidate density; bypass block-pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap
precheck candidate density; bypass block-pruning path when all 600 blocks overlap

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current plan is logically wrong for this data because the independent 100000-row zone maps prune zero blocks, so the block-candidate pipeline adds metadata work, dynamic scheduling overhead, and branch-heavy per-block specialization without eliminating any scan work; the prior bitmap/compaction attempt also regressed by turning a bandwidth problem into extra write-heavy passes. Strategy Category A: replace both paths with one fused, bandwidth-oriented scan over contiguous row ranges that streams the four fixed-width lineitem columns in lockstep, evaluates all predicates inline, and accumulates directly into thread-local scalar sums; keep the tiny zone maps only as a cheap 600-block density precheck so the executor can fall back to pruning only when overlap is sparse, but on this query's hot path it should bypass indexes entirely and maximize sequential memory throughput.
